params.name: filter_id
params.description: If supplied, only activities matching the specified filter are returned

params.name: ids
params.description: Optional comma separated string array of up to 100 entity ids to fetch. If filter_id is provided, this is ignored. If any of the requested entities do not exist or are not visible, they are not included in the response.

params.name: owner_id
params.description: If supplied, only activities owned by the specified user are returned. If filter_id is provided, this is ignored.

params.name: deal_id
params.description: If supplied, only activities linked to the specified deal are returned. If filter_id is provided, this is ignored.

params.name: lead_id
params.description: If supplied, only activities linked to the specified lead are returned. If filter_id is provided, this is ignored.

params.name: person_id
params.description: If supplied, only activities whose primary participant is the given person are returned. If filter_id is provided, this is ignored.

params.name: org_id
params.description: If supplied, only activities linked to the specified organization are returned. If filter_id is provided, this is ignored.

params.name: done
params.description: If supplied, only activities with specified 'done' flag value are returned

params.name: updated_since
params.description: If set, only activities with an `update_time` later than or equal to this time are returned. In RFC3339 format, e.g. 2025-01-01T10:20:00Z.

params.name: updated_until
params.description: If set, only activities with an `update_time` earlier than this time are returned. In RFC3339 format, e.g. 2025-01-01T10:20:00Z.

params.name: sort_by
params.description: The field to sort by. Supported fields: `id`, `update_time`, `add_time`, `due_date`.

params.name: sort_direction
params.description: The sorting direction. Supported values: `asc`, `desc`.

params.name: include_fields
params.description: Optional comma separated string array of additional fields to include

params.name: limit
params.description: For pagination, the limit of entries to be returned. If not provided, 100 items will be returned. Please note that a maximum value of 500 is allowed.

params.name: cursor
params.description: For pagination, the marker (an opaque string value) representing the first item on the next page

params.name: status
params.description: Only fetch deals with a specific status. If omitted, all not deleted deals are returned. If set to deleted, deals that have been deleted up to 30 days ago will be included. Multiple statuses can be included as a comma separated array. If filter_id is provided, this is ignored.

params.description: If set, only deals with an `update_time` later than or equal to this time are returned. In RFC3339 format, e.g. 2025-01-01T10:20:00Z.

params.name: updated_until
params.description: If set, only deals with an `update_time` earlier than this time are returned. In RFC3339 format, e.g. 2025-01-01T10:20:00Z.

params.description: Optional comma separated string array of additional fields to include

params.name: custom_fields
params.description:
Optional comma separated string array of custom fields keys to include. If you are only interested in a particular set of custom fields, please use this parameter for faster results and smaller response.
A maximum of 15 keys is allowed.

params.name: include_option_labels
params.description: When provided with a 'true' value, single option and multiple option custom fields values contain objects in the form of '{ id: number, label: string }' instead of plain id

params.name: include_labels
params.description: When provided with 'true' value, response will include an array of label objects in the form of '{ id: number, label: string }'

params.name: term
params.description: The search term to look for. Minimum 2 characters (or 1 if using `exact_match`). Please note that the search term has to be URL encoded.

params.name: fields
params.description: A comma-separated string array. The fields to perform the search from. Defaults to all of them. Only the following custom field types are searchable: `address`, `varchar`, `text`, `varchar_auto`, `double`, `monetary` and `phone`. Read more about searching by custom fields here.

params.name: exact_match
params.description: When enabled, only full exact matches against the given term are returned. It is not case sensitive.

params.name: person_id
params.description: Will filter deals by the provided person ID. The upper limit of found deals associated with the person is 2000.

params.name: organization_id
params.description: Will filter deals by the provided organization ID. The upper limit of found deals associated with the organization is 2000.

params.name: status
params.description: Will filter deals by the provided specific status. open = Open, won = Won, lost = Lost. The upper limit of found deals associated with the status is 2000.

params.name: include_fields
params.description: Supports including optional fields in the results which are not provided by default

params.name: limit
params.description: For pagination, the limit of entries to be returned. If not provided, 100 items will be returned. Please note that a maximum value of 500 is allowed.

params.name: cursor
params.description: For pagination, the marker (an opaque string value) representing the first item on the next page

items.docs: Searches all deals by title, notes and/or custom fields. This endpoint is a wrapper of /v1/itemSearch with a narrower OAuth scope. Found deals can be filtered by the person ID and the organization ID.

params.name: id
params.description: The ID of the deal

params.name: discount_id
params.description: The ID of the discount

items.docs: Removes a discount from a deal, changing the deal value if the deal has one-time products attached.

info.name: Convert a deal to a lead

params.name: id
params.description: The ID of the deal to convert

items.docs: Initiates a conversion of a deal to a lead. The return value is an ID of a job that was assigned to perform the conversion. Related entities (notes, files, emails, activities, ...) are transferred during the process to the target entity. There are exceptions for entities like invoices or history that are not transferred and remain linked to the original deal. If the conversion is successful, the deal is marked as deleted. To retrieve the created entity ID and the result of the conversion, call t

params.name: id
params.description: The ID of a deal

params.name: conversion_id
params.description: The ID of the conversion

items.docs: Returns information about the conversion. Status is always present and its value (not_started, running, completed, failed, rejected) represents the current state of the conversion. Lead ID is only present if the conversion was successfully finished. This data is only temporary and removed after a few days.

Lists installments attached to a list of deals.

Only available in Growth and above plans.

info.name: Add an installment to a deal

params.name: id
params.description: The ID of the deal

items.docs:
Adds an installment to a deal.

An installment can only be added if the deal includes at least one one-time product. 
If the deal contains at least one recurring product, adding installments is not allowed.

Only available in Growth and above plans.

Only available in Growth and above plans.

params.description: The ID of the deal

params.name: installment_id
params.description: The ID of the installment

items.docs:
Removes an installment from a deal.

Only available in Growth and above plans.

info.name: Add deal field options in bulk

params.name: field_code
params.description: The unique code identifying the field

items.docs: Adds new options to a deal custom field that supports options (enum or set field types). This operation is atomic - all options are added or none are added. Returns only the newly added options.

info.name: Update deal field options in bulk

params.name: field_code
params.description: The unique code identifying the field

items.docs: Updates existing options for a deal custom field. This operation is atomic and fails if any of the specified option IDs do not exist. Returns only the updated options.

info.name: Delete deal field options in bulk

params.name: field_code
params.description: The unique code identifying the field

items.docs: Removes existing options from a deal custom field. This operation is atomic and fails if any of the specified option IDs do not exist. Returns only the deleted options.

params.name: filter_id
params.description: If supplied, only organizations matching the specified filter are returned

params.name: ids
params.description: Optional comma separated string array of up to 100 entity ids to fetch. If filter_id is provided, this is ignored. If any of the requested entities do not exist or are not visible, they are not included in the response.

params.name: owner_id
params.description: If supplied, only organizations owned by the specified user are returned. If filter_id is provided, this is ignored.

params.name: updated_since
params.description: If set, only organizations with an `update_time` later than or equal to this time are returned. In RFC3339 format, e.g. 2025-01-01T10:20:00Z.

params.name: updated_until
params.description: If set, only organizations with an `update_time` earlier than this time are returned. In RFC3339 format, e.g. 2025-01-01T10:20:00Z.

params.name: sort_by
params.description: The field to sort by. Supported fields: `id`, `update_time`, `add_time`.

params.name: sort_direction
params.description: The sorting direction. Supported values: `asc`, `desc`.

params.name: include_fields
params.description: Optional comma separated string array of additional fields to include

params.name: custom_fields
params.description:
Optional comma separated string array of custom fields keys to include. If you are only interested in a particular set of custom fields, please use this parameter for faster results and smaller response.
A maximum of 15 keys is allowed.

params.name: include_option_labels
params.description: When provided with a 'true' value, single option and multiple option custom fields values contain objects in the form of '{ id: number, label: string }' instead of plain id

params.name: include_labels
params.description: When provided with 'true' value, response will include an array of label objects in the form of '{ id: number, label: string }'

params.name: limit
params.description: For pagination, the limit of entries to be returned. If not provided, 100 items will be returned. Please note that a maximum value of 500 is allowed.

params.name: cursor
params.description: For pagination, the marker (an opaque string value) representing the first item on the next page

items.docs: Searches all leads by title, notes and/or custom fields. This endpoint is a wrapper of /v1/itemSearch with a narrower OAuth scope. Found leads can be filtered by the person ID and the organization ID.

info.name: Convert a lead to a deal

params.name: id
params.description: The ID of the lead to convert

items.docs: Initiates a conversion of a lead to a deal. The return value is an ID of a job that was assigned to perform the conversion. Related entities (notes, files, emails, activities, ...) are transferred during the process to the target entity. If the conversion is successful, the lead is marked as deleted. To retrieve the created entity ID and the result of the conversion, call the <a href="https://developers.pipedrive.com/docs/api/v1/Leads#getLeadConversionStatus">/api/v2/leads/{lead_id}/convert/stat

params.name: id
params.description: The ID of a lead

params.name: conversion_id
params.description: The ID of the conversion

items.docs: Returns data about the conversion. Status is always present and its value (not_started, running, completed, failed, rejected) represents the current state of the conversion. Deal ID is only present if the conversion was successfully finished. This data is only temporary and removed after a few days.

info.name: Perform a search from multiple item types

params.name: term
params.description: The search term to look for. Minimum 2 characters (or 1 if using `exact_match`). Please note that the search term has to be URL encoded.

params.name: item_types
params.description: A comma-separated string array. The type of items to perform the search from. Defaults to all.

params.name: fields
params.description:
A comma-separated string array. The fields to perform the search from. Defaults to all. Relevant for each item type are:
Item typeField
Deal`custom_fields`, `notes`, `title`
Person`custom_fields`, `email`, `name`, `notes`, `phone`
Organization`address`, `custom_fields`, `name`, `notes`
Product`code`, `custom_fields`, `name`
Lead`custom_fields`, `notes`, `title`
File`name`
Mail attachment`name`
Project `custom_fields`, `notes`, `title`, `description`

Only the following custom field types are searchable: `address`, `varchar`, `text`, `varchar_auto`, `double`, `monetary` and `phone`. Read more about searching by custom fields here.

params.name: search_for_related_items
params.description: When enabled, the response will include up to 100 newest related leads and 100 newest related deals for each found person and organization and up to 100 newest related persons for each found organization

params.name: exact_match
params.description: When enabled, only full exact matches against the given term are returned. It is not case sensitive.

params.name: include_fields
params.description: A comma-separated string array. Supports including optional fields in the results which are not provided by default.

params.name: limit
params.description: For pagination, the limit of entries to be returned. If not provided, 100 items will be returned. Please note that a maximum value of 100 is allowed.

params.name: cursor
params.description: For pagination, the marker (an opaque string value) representing the first item on the next page

items.docs: Performs a search from your choice of item types and fields.

info.name: Perform a search using a specific field from an item type

params.name: term
params.description: The search term to look for. Minimum 2 characters (or 1 if `match` is `exact`). Please note that the search term has to be URL encoded.

params.name: entity_type
params.description: The type of the field to perform the search from

params.name: match
params.description:
The type of match used against the term. The search is case sensitive.

E.g. in case of searching for a value `monkey`,
  • with `exact` match, you will only find it if term is `monkey`
  • with `beginning` match, you will only find it if the term matches the beginning or the whole string, e.g. `monk` and `monkey`
  • with `middle` match, you will find the it if the term matches any substring of the value, e.g. `onk` and `ke`
.

params.name: field
params.description: The key of the field to search from. The field key can be obtained by fetching the list of the fields using any of the fields' API GET methods (dealFields, personFields, etc.). Only the following custom field types are searchable: `address`, `varchar`, `text`, `varchar_auto`, `double`, `monetary` and `phone`. Read more about searching by custom fields here.

params.name: limit
params.description: For pagination, the limit of entries to be returned. If not provided, 100 items will be returned. Please note that a maximum value of 500 is allowed.

params.name: cursor
params.description: For pagination, the marker (an opaque string value) representing the first item on the next page

items.docs: Performs a search from the values of a specific field. Results can either be the distinct values of the field (useful for searching autocomplete field values), or the IDs of actual items (deals, leads, persons, organizations or products).

params.name: cursor
params.description: For pagination, the marker (an opaque string value) representing the first item on the next page

params.name: limit
params.description: For pagination, the limit of entries to be returned. If not provided, 100 items will be returned. Please note that a maximum value of 500 is allowed.

params.name: is_done
params.description: Whether the task is done or not. If omitted, both done and not done tasks are returned.

params.name: is_milestone
params.description: Whether the task is a milestone or not. If omitted, both milestone and non-milestone tasks are returned.

params.name: assignee_id
params.description: If supplied, only tasks assigned to this user are returned

params.name: project_id
params.description: If supplied, only tasks belonging to this project are returned

params.name: parent_task_id
params.description: If `null` is supplied, only root-level tasks (without a parent) are returned. If an integer is supplied, only subtasks of that specific task are returned. By default all tasks are returned.

items.docs: Returns all tasks.
Adds a new task.

params.name: id
params.description: The ID of the task

params.name: id
params.description: The ID of the task

params.name: id
params.description: The ID of the task

items.docs: Marks a task as deleted. If the task has subtasks, those will also be deleted.
